def: adj. Incorporeal; unembodied.

s.m. = اسپرمول isparmūl, and اشرمول iśarmūl, q.q.v. s.m. The plant Indian birthwort, Aristolochia Indica. (It is said to be a valuable antidote to snake-bites, being applied both externally and internally
adj. Poor; insignificant.
s.f. Poverty; insignificance.

part. adj. Resorting (to); dependent (on); — s.m. One who has recourse to or relies on another; refugee; protegée; dependant, follower, subject, servant, retainer; hanger on, parasite, sycophant; — a Brahman who assists in the performance of marriage ceremonies.
s.f. Foundation; pedestal.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اساس (asās), from Arabic أَسَاس (ʔasās).

ety: quasi-pl. of عاسّ, v.n. fr. عسّ 'to go round about, to patrol'
def: s.m. The patrol, night-watch, guards.
s.m. Goods, household goods, chattels, effects, property generally (syn. متاع matāʻ).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اثاث (asās), from Arabic أَثَاث (ʔaṯāṯ).

def: s.m. Goods, household goods, chattels, effects, property generally (syn. متاع matāʻ).
adj. Contrary to or inconsistent with the rules of science or of law; unlawful, illegal, irregular, inconsistent; unscriptural, heterodox, erroneous.
adj. Contrary to or inconsistent with the rules of science or of law; unlawful, illegal, irregular, inconsistent; unscriptural, heterodox, erroneous.
adj. Untrue, unreal, unfounded, false; fictitious, not genuine, counterfeit, delusive, deceptive, illusory; unholy, ungodly, irreligious, impious, unjust, unfair
subs: asat phāṅknā, To tell lies; to fabricate, to invent
v.t. To commit an injustice; to do wrong, evil, &c.

def: adj. Addicted to bad company.
s.m. Upstart; stranger, unknown person.
s.m. pl. (of استاذ ustāẕ), Teachers.
s.f. Intimate union, connection; juxtaposition; (in Gram.) general harmony in a sentence (grammatically or otherwise).
s.m.f. Powerless, helpless; — a powerless or helpless person, a poor creature; indolent person.
s.m. One who speaks falsehood, a liar.
adj. & s.m. Untrue, false; — a liar.
s.m. Impurity, foulness; social or legal uncleanness (as from the death of a relation, &c.); mourning.
s.m. Impurity, &c. = اسوچ asauć, q.v. s.m. Impurity, foulness; social or legal uncleanness (as from the death of a relation, &c.); mourning.
s.m. Rum, spirit distilled from sugar or molasses, spirituous liquor in general.
adj. Careless, inadvertent, inattentive, negligent.
s.f. Carelessness, inattention, negligence.
s.m. A careless or negligent person.
adj. Hopeful; desiring; expecting; dependent; — s.m. (f. vatī), A candidate ( = ummedwār), expectant; dependent.
adj. Timid, fearful; unquiet; pitiful.
adj. Hopeful, expectant; dependent.
adj. = اشانت aśānt, q.v. part. adj. Unappeased; unresigned, restless, anxious, disquieted, fearful, apprehensive.
adj. & s.m. = آشاوان āśā-vān, q.v. adj. Hopeful; desiring; expecting; dependent; — s.m. (f. vatī), A candidate ( = ummedwār), expectant; dependent.
adj. & s.m. = آشاوان āśā-vān, q.v. adj. Hopeful; desiring; expecting; dependent; — s.m. (f. vatī), A candidate ( = ummedwār), expectant; dependent.
s.f. Name of a rāginī or musical mode; a kind of tune; — a kind of pigeon; — a kind of silken and silver cloth.
= اساوري asāvarī, q.v. s.f. Name of a rāginī or musical mode; a kind of tune; — a kind of pigeon; — a kind of silken and silver cloth.
s.m. = آسي āsay, q.v. s.m. Resting-place, seat, place; asylum, abode, retreat; receptacle; meaning, intention; aim, scope; theme, subject.
s.m. Resting-place, seat, place; asylum, abode, retreat; receptacle; meaning, intention; aim, scope; theme, subject.
s.m. Implements of trade
subs: asbāb-i-ḥarb, s.m. Military stores, warlike provisions or necessaries, munitions of war, arms and ammunition
s.m. Travelling requisites; luggage or provisions for a journey.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic أَسْبَاب (ʔasbāb). Compare Bengali আসবাব (asbab).

def: s.m. Past times, days of yore.
s.m. pl. (of سبط sibt̤), Tribes (especially of Israel).
s.m. Matter of wonder; wonder, amazement, astonishment, surprise; — adj. Astonishing, wonderful
subs: (or āśćarj) karnā, v.t. To be astonished or surprised, to marvel.

def: s.m. Matter of wonder; wonder, amazement, astonishment, surprise; — adj. Astonishing, wonderful
subs: (or āśćarj) karnā, v.t. To be astonished or surprised, to marvel.
wety: From Sanskrit आश्चर्य (āścarya).

def: adj. Astonishing, wonderful; extraordinary; — s.m. A wonderful person, a prodigy.
s.f. Wonderfulness, wonder, astonishment.
adj. Astonishing, wonderful; extraordinary; — s.m. A wonderful person, a prodigy.
adj. Astonishing, wonderful; extraordinary; — s.m. A wonderful person, a prodigy.
part. adj. Astonished, amazed, astounded.
s.m. Misfortune, trouble, calamity (especially such as is the consequence of the shadow of a Jinn or demon having fallen on one); harm, injury, damage, hurt; apprehension, fear
subs: To bring trouble (upon); to injure, damage, hurt
To suffer harm, &c.; to be injured or hurt
āseb utārnā, To remove or cure of disease, &c. caused by the shadow of a Jinn or evil spirit; to expel an evil spirit, to exorcise.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آسیب (āsēb).

def: s.m.f. One who is afflicted, or in trouble or calamity; one who is under the influence of a Jinn or evil spirit.
adj. = اسيش asesh, q.v. adj. Without remainder; all, entire.
s.m. An assessor, a jury-man.
adj. = اسيش asesh, q.v. adj. Without remainder; all, entire.
adj. Without remainder; all, entire.

s.m. = S اسيوا असेवा asevā, s.f. Disregard, inattention, neglect, disobedience.
adj. compar. & superl. (of سافل sāfil), Lower, inferior; lowest, nethermost
adj. Yellow; saffron-coloured.
wety: From Arabic أَصْفَر (ʔaṣfar).

def: s.m. pl. (of سفر safar), Voyages, travels, adventures.
s. pl. (of سفر sifr), Books, writings, volumes, registers.
s.m. pl. (of صفر ṣifr), Ciphers.
s.m. pl. (of صفی), The pure, the just. the holy; saints.
s.m. Name of a medicinal plant, Physalis flexuosa.
adj. compar. & superl. (of صغير ṣag̠īr), Less, least, smallest; very little; — the minor term in a logical proposition.

ety: = S. आश. rt. Zend aś = S. अश्
def: s.f. Meat, victuals, viands; soup, broth, gruel, pottage; paste, starch, size ( = mānd)
subs: (-kī), To make a paste or jelly (of), to beat to a jelly; to combine in a design (against), to conspire or plot against
s.f. A pulāʼo of ground barley (given to sick persons)

ety: v.n. fr. عشو 'to eat the evening meal'
def: s.m. The evening meal, supper
subs: or ʻashā-ě-rabbānī, s.m. The Lord's supper.

def: s.m. Learned men, the learned
subs: The great companions (of Moḥammad), the four Khalifs immediately succeeding Moḥammad
The chosen companions (of the Prophet) = aṣḥāb-i-kibār
The companions of the Cave, the Seven Sleepers (famed in Oriental romance).
wety: From Arabic أَصْحَاب (ʔaṣḥāb).

def: adj. compar. & superl. (of شديد shadīd), More vehement; most vehement; very violent or severe; severe, violent, excessive.
adj. compar. & superl. (of سہل sahl), More easy; most easy.
adj. Having a disjointed, withered or paralytic hand.
part. act. Drinking, drinker (used in comp. e.g. sharāb-āshām, 'wine-drinker').
s.m.f. pl. (of صہر ṣěhr), Relations by marriage (within the prohibited degrees).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَصْهَار (ashār), from Arabic أَصْهَار (ʔaṣhār).

def: s.m. pl. (of شعر sheʻr), Verses, poems.

ety: for A. عشرة ʻasharat; see ʻashr
def: adj. & s.m. Ten; — a decade; ten days; the first ten days of Moḥarram (see dahā, and ʻāshūrā).
s.m. Decimal numbers, tens.
= H اشاڙها अषाढ़ा ashāṛhā, = S. आषाढ; see اساڙهہ asāṛh, the com. form.
= H اشاڙهہ अषाढ़ ashāṛh, = S. आषाढ; see اساڙهہ asāṛh, the com. form.
adj. compar. & superl. (of شرير sharīr), More, most, or very wicked or vicious; most malignant; wicked, vicious, malignant; atrocious.
wety: From Sanskrit अश्रु (áśru), from Proto-Indo-European *dáḱru-. Cognate with Ancient Greek δάκρυ (dákru), Lithuanian ašara.

ety: for orig. اش ash, redupl.; cf. ḥashāsh
def: s.m. Rejoicing, &c.
subs: (-se), To rejoice (at), be greatly pleased (with); to admire, praise, applaud.

def: s.m. pl. (of شبح shabaḥ), Bodies, objects.
s.f. pl. (of شُعاع shǒʻāʻ, rt. شعّ), Rays of light.
s.m. pl. (of شفق shafaq), Kindnesses, favours.
wety: From Classical Persian اشفاق (ašfāq, išfāq), from Arabic إِشْفَاق (ʔišfāq), verbal noun of أَشْفَقَ (ʔašfaqa), and أَشْفَاق (ʔašfāq), the plural of شَفَق (šafaq).

def: s.m. pl. (of شغل shag̠l), Occupations, employments, labours, cares.
adj. Grey, ash-coloured; of a black and white colour.
s.m. pl. (of شاهد shāhid), Witnesses, eye-witnesses.
(1st pers. sing. aor. of شہد), I bear witness, I testify, I declare.
adj. compar. & superl. (of شہير shahīr = mashhūr), More, and most, apparent, notorious, celebrated, &c.; better, and best, known.

ety: act. part. of عشق 'to love passionately,' &c.
def: part. adj. & s.m. Loving passionately, amorous, enamoured, in love; — a passionate lover, a lover, an inamorato
subs: adj. 'Having the temper or disposition of a lover,' sprightly, sportive, gay, merry
Lover and beloved, lovers; — a snake-belt
(-par), To be enamoured (of), to be, or to fall, in love (with), to admire.
wety: First attested in c. 1503 as Middle Hindi عاشِق ('aśq), borrowed from Classical Persian عَاشِق (āšiq), from Arabic عَاشِق (ʕāšiq).

ety: for A. عاشقة, fem. of ʻāshiq
def: s.f. An inamorato, a sweetheart, mistress.
s.m. pl. (of ʻāshiq), Lovers.

ety: ʻāshiq, q.v.+Zend aff. āna or ana = S. अन
def: adj. & adv. Lover-like, loving, amorous, erotic, love- (e.g. ʻāshiqānā ḵẖat̤, 'a love-letter'); — amorously; after the manner of a lover.

ety: ʻāshiq, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Making love, amorousness, love, courtship; gallantry; the being in love, the state or condition of a lover
s.m. A tenth part (i.q. ʻushr, q.v.). s.m. A tenth part, a tithe
subs: The tithes prescribed by divine law
The tenth of a tenth, a hundredth part; (fig.) very little.

def: adj. Tenth.
adj. Tenth.

ety: Zend ā+shayana, rt. khshi = S. क्षि
def: s.m. Nest (of a bird, &c.).

ety: Zend ā+shayana, rt. khshi = S. क्षि
def: s.m. Nest (of a bird, &c.).
adj. compar. & superl. (of شجاع shujāʻ), Braver; bravest; more, or most, valiant; brave, valiant.
s.m. pl. (of شجر shajar), Trees.
s.m. A tear; tears; — ashk-afshān, ashk-bār, part. act. Shedding tears, weeping, mourning
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اشک (ašk) from Proto-Iranian *Hácru, from Proto-Indo-European *h₂éḱru (“tear”). Doublet of آن٘سُو (ā̃sū) and اشر (aśru).

def: s.f. pl. (of شکل shakl), Forms, figures, shapes, appearances, semblances.

ety: Pehl. ashkārak, Zend āka; cf. āgāh
def: adj. Apparent, manifest, clear, plain, open, public, known, revealed
subs: v.t. To make manifest, clear or plain, to explain; to unfold, to make known; to reveal; to declare, disclose, divulge; to make public; to expose
v.n. To be made known, be revealed; to be unfolded; to become public; to be exposed.

ety: Pehl. ashkārak, Zend āka; cf. āgāh
def: adj. Apparent, manifest, clear, plain, open, public, known, revealed
subs: v.t. To make manifest, clear or plain, to explain; to unfold, to make known; to reveal; to declare, disclose, divulge; to make public; to expose
v.n. To be made known, be revealed; to be unfolded; to become public; to be exposed.

def: s.m. pl. (of شخص shaḵẖṣ), Persons, people.
s.m. pl. (of شقی shaqī), Thieves, malefactors.
adj. compar. & superl. (of شامل shāmil), More, most, or very comprehensive; more perfect; most perfect or complete; surpassing, transcendent.
adj. Acquainted (with, -se), knowing, known; attached (to), fond (of)
subs: adj. Friendly, attentive to friends, sociable, obliging
s.m.f. An apparent or outward friend
v.n. To become very friendly; to become tame or domesticated (an animal; syn. hil-jānā).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آشْنَا (āšnā).

def: s.f. Acquaintance, friendship, intimacy, familiarity; connection, relationship; connection by marriage; illicit love, carnal intercourse
subs: āshnāʼī karnā (-se), To make acquaintance (with), make friends (with), strike up a friendship (with); to form an attachment or an intimacy (with); to form an unlawful attachment or connection (with)
āshnāʼī honā, v.n. To become friends (with), become intimate (with), to be united in friendship (with).

def: adv. In a friendly manner; through friendship; like a friend.
s.m. Tumult, clamour; storm, tempest; terror; misfortune; inflammation of the eyes; an impostor; — part. adj. (used in comp.) exciting.
adj. f. (m. ʻasharat; see ʻashara), Ten.
(for sharābāt), s.f. pl. Drinks prohibited by the Moḥammadan law, such as wines, &c. (see sharāb).
adj. Well-born, of good family, noble; gentle, meek, mild; refined, courteous, urbane.
wety: From Arabic أَشْرَاف (ʔašrāf).

def: s.m. A person of noble birth, nobleman; gentleman
subs: The noblest or most excellent of created things, mankind

ety: abst. subst. from ashrāf, and = A. sharāfat, q.v.
def: s.f. Nobility, nobleness; gentleness, affability, urbanity, civility, courtesy, good-breeding, politeness.

ety: said to be so called from Ashraf, the king in whose reign the coin obtained currency
def: s.f. Name of a gold coin, a gold-mohar; (the value of a Calcutta gold-mohar is about 16 rupees).
adj. & s.m. Wicked, wretched, criminal, seditious; — the wicked, &c.
adj. Eight ( = H. āṭh)
subs: (S. ashṭan+anga), s.m. Eight parts of the body with which very profound obeisance is performed (viz. the hands, thighs, breast, eyes, and forehead); — adj. With, or consisting of, eight members or parts
(ashṭāng-parnām), s.m. Prostration in adoration or salutation by which the ground is touched with the eight principal parts of the body
adj. Possessed of, or having, eight arms
( = āṭhoṅ pahar), ashṭa-jām, adv. Through the eight watches, night and day, always, incessantly
s.m. The eight metals; bell-metal, mixed metal ( = azhdhāt)
s.m. Anything consisting of a combination of the eight metals
ashṭa-dhāvī, s.f. A compound of the eight metals, &c. ( = ashṭa-dhātu)

s.m. The eight siddhis or superior beings, personifications of the powers and laws of nature: (when those powers are subjected to the will by holiness and austerity, whatever the fancy desires may, it is said, be obtained; even universal sovereignty may be acquired and implicit obedience to any command enforced; the magnitude or weight of the body may be increased or diminished at will, or the body transferred in an instant to any part of the universe)
s.m. Lord of the eight worlds

s.m. A norse with a white face, tail, breast, and hoofs; a collection of eight lucky things to be assembled on certain occasions (as a lion, a bull, an elephant, a water-jar, a fan, a flag, a trumpet and a lamp; or a Brahman, a cow, fire, gold, ghee, the sun, water, and a king).

def: s.m. One eighth; — adj. Eight-fold.
adj. Eighth; — s.m. An eighth.
s.f. The eighth day (night) in a (lunar) half-month. (See janam-ashṭamī.)
= آفتابد āftāba, q.v.

ety: Pehl. āshtis, āshtia, Zend ākhsti, rt. śtā = S. स्था
def: s.f. Peace, concord, reconciliation, agreement, accommodation, convention, confederacy; amour, intrigue
= اشٿهي ashṭhī, q.v.

ety: perf. part. of āshuftan; ā+rt. Zend. khshub = S. kshub
def: part. adj. Distracted, disturbed, distressed; disordered; uneasy, wretched, miserable; enamoured, deeply in love
subs: adj. Distressed in condition, embarrassed; distracted, &c. ( = āshufta)
s.f. Distress of condition, embarrassment; state of distraction, wretchedness, &c.
āshufta-dil, adj. Distressed or afflicted in mind, disquieted, uneasy
āshufta-sarī, s.f. Distraction of mind, or of the senses, derangement of the mind, madness
āshufta-mū, adj. With disordered or dishevelled hair; shaggy
āshufta-damāg̠, āshufta-sar, adj. Distracted in mind, head or brain

def: s.f. Distraction, perturbation, uneasiness; misery.
s.m. The tenth day, or the first ten days, of the month Moharram
subs: s.m. A temporary structure for the celebration of the Moḥarram festival; a house for the reception of the banners, &c. used in the Moḥarram procession.

def: s.m. = ʻāshūra, q.v. s.m. The tenth day, or the first ten days, of the month Moharram
subs: s.m. A temporary structure for the celebration of the Moḥarram festival; a house for the reception of the banners, &c. used in the Moḥarram procession.

def: s.m. The tenth day, or the first ten days, of the month Moharram
subs: s.m. A temporary structure for the celebration of the Moḥarram festival; a house for the reception of the banners, &c. used in the Moḥarram procession.

ety: for A. عشوة, v.n. fr. عشو; see ʻishā, and ʻashā
def: s.m. Darkness, the first of the darkness (of the night); — a confused and dubious affair, or venture, or undertaking, an obscure or concealed affair; a dangerous affair; — a fire seen from a distance at night; a beacon; — (met.) coquetry, ogling, blandishments, amorous playfulness
subs: s.m. & f. One who ogles; a coquette

def: s.f. pl. (of شي shai), Things, effects, chattels.
s.m. Sword, scimitar. (Syn. کهڙگ khaṛg)
subs: s.m. Blade of a sword or scimitar; scabbard; a hell paved with swords; a sugar-cane.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀏𑀲𑁄 (eso), from Sanskrit एषः (eṣaḥ).

ety: for A. عاصٍ ʻāṣiṉ, act. part. of عصي 'to disobey, to rebel'
def: adj. & s.m. Disobedient, rebellious; criminal; — a rebel; a sinner.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عاصی ('āsī), from Arabic عَاصٍ (ʕāṣin).

def: = اس asi, q.v. s.m. Sword, scimitar. (Syn. کهڙگ khaṛg)
subs: s.m. Blade of a sword or scimitar; scabbard; a hell paved with swords; a sugar-cane.

ety: अ+H. सीचा, perf. part. of सीचना; S. rt. सिच्
def: adj. Not watered, not irrigated.
adj. Not effected, unaccomplished, imperfect, incomplete; unripe, raw, not properly cooked; invalid; false.
s.m. Blessing, benediction; admonition, instruction.
part. adj. Untaught, illiterate, ignorant.
adj. Ill-behaved; bad-natured.

adj. Of good stock, lineage or pedigree, of good family, well-born, noble; of pure breed; game (cock); gentle, free from vice; high-spirited; — s.f. A maid-servant who is free (in contr. to launḍī a 'purchased slave').
wety: Borrowed from Arabic أَصِيل (ʔaṣīl).

def: adj. & s.m. Sinning, doing what is unlawful; sinner, criminal; — a great and habitual liar.

ety: act. part. of عصم 'to protect, or defend,' &c.
def: part. adj. & s.m. Defending, protecting; protected, safe; virtuous, chaste; — a defender, &c.
subs: 'Father-safe,' an appellation of the meal of parched barley or the like, and also of barley broth.

def: adj. Without limits or boundaries; unlimited, unbounded, boundless.
adj. Amazed, astonished, bewildered, confused, confounded (e.g. sar-āsīma, bewildered in the head, astounded).

ety: for A. عاصمة, fem. of ʻāṣim
def: adj. & s.f. Virtuous, chaste; — a chaste woman.
s.m. The sixth month of the Hindūs (Sept.-Oct.), during which the moon is full near the stars at the head of Aries.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit आसन (āsana).

def: adj. Seated, sitting.
adj. = اسيچا asīćā, q.v. adj. Not watered, not irrigated.
adj. Marking; marked, favoured; chosen; — s.m. (Gr. αἰθηρ), Ether
subs: s.m., The ether, the ninth and greatest of the spheres.

def: part. adj. Bound, tied, made captive; — s.m. Prisoner, captive; — asīr-i-āb-o-gil, Bound or attached to (one's) native soil.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَسِیر (asīr), from Arabic أَسِير (ʔasīr).

ety: v.n. and inf. n. of عسر 'to be difficult,' &c.
def: adj. Difficult, hard; — ʻasaruʼl-ʻubūr, Difficult to cross or pass over.

s.m. = اسيرباد asīrbād, q.v.
s.m. Blessing, &c. = اسيرباد asīr-bād, q.v.

def: s.m. Blessing, benediction; salutation, greeting ( = āsir-bād)
subs: (-ko), asīr-bād karnā, v.t. To bestow a blessing (on), pronounce a benediction (on), to bless; to salute, greet; to make obeisance to.

def: s.f. Imprisonment, captivity.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَسِیرِی (asīrī). By surface analysis, اَسِیر (asīr) + ی (ī).

def: s.f. = اسيس asīs, q.v. s.f. Prayer; blessing, benediction; return of compliments from a (Hindū) superior; reward, prize
subs: (-ko), To bestow or pray for a blessing (upon), to invoke a blessing (upon), to bless; to salute, greet; to make obeisance (to) = asīr-bād denā.

def: s.f. Prayer; blessing, benediction; return of compliments from a (Hindū) superior; reward, prize
subs: (-ko), To bestow or pray for a blessing (upon), to invoke a blessing (upon), to bless; to salute, greet; to make obeisance (to) = asīr-bād denā.

def: s.f. Prayer; blessing, benediction; return of compliments from a (Hindū) superior; reward, prize
subs: (-ko), To bestow or pray for a blessing (upon), to invoke a blessing (upon), to bless; to salute, greet; to make obeisance (to) = asīr-bād denā.

def: s.f. = آشس āśis, q.v.
s.f. = آشس āśis, q.v.
adj. Untrained, badly-trained, ill-regulated, ill-behaved, unpolite, rude, barbarous; indecent, profligate.
part. adj. = اسشٿ asishta, q.v. adj. Untrained, badly-trained, ill-regulated, ill-behaved, unpolite, rude, barbarous; indecent, profligate.
v.t. = asīs-denā, q.v.s.v. اسيس asīs.
adj. Black; — s.m. The colour black, the dark half of the month from full to new moon.
adj. Unlucky, inauspicious; not prosperous, unhappy; pernicious, dangerous.
adj. = اسو asiv, q.v. adj. Unlucky, inauspicious; not prosperous, unhappy; pernicious, dangerous.

ety: comp. and super. of ثقيل s̤aqīl
def: adj. Heavier; heaviest, very heavy.
s.m. An army, a force, troop.
adj. & s.m. Of or relating to an army, military; — a soldier.
s.f. Inactivity, remissness; dulness; tardiness, dilatoriness; slothfulness, laziness, indolence; heaviness, drowsiness, sleepiness; procrastination.
s.f. Inactivity, remissness; dulness; tardiness, dilatoriness; slothfulness, laziness, indolence; heaviness, drowsiness, sleepiness; procrastination.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit आसक्त (āsakta).

def: v.n. To be inactive; to be lazy, idle, &c.; to be disinclined to work; to be idle; to move slowly, to dawdle, dally, to be dilatory; to take it easy.
v.n. To be inactive; to be lazy, idle, &c.; to be disinclined to work; to be idle; to move slowly, to dawdle, dally, to be dilatory; to take it easy.
adj. Inactive, doing nothing, lazy, sluggish, indolent; remiss; slow, tardy, dilatory, backward; drowsy, dozy, sleepy; — s.m. A lazy fellow, idler, sluggard; dawdler, loiterer, &c.
adj. Inactive, doing nothing, lazy, sluggish, indolent; remiss; slow, tardy, dilatory, backward; drowsy, dozy, sleepy; — s.m. A lazy fellow, idler, sluggard; dawdler, loiterer, &c.

s.f. Bottom, root, origin, base, foundation; original, source; an essential, a fundamental principle; essence; element, principle; chief thing, main point, original or old state or condition; original or primary signification; original copy (of a work or document); a radical letter (as distinguished from an augmentative); reality, fact, truth; race, stock, lineage; a man of good stock, a thorough-bred gentleman; capital, stock-in-trade, principal; — adj. Radical, original, fundamental, &c. = aṣlī, q.v.
subs: adv. ( = dar aṣl), In fact, in effect, in reality, really, actually; positively, absolutely
s.m. Real name (in contr. to ʻurf or alias)
s.m. Real or clear gain, net profit
s.f. Root and branch; cause and effect
The fact of the matter is this, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَصْل (asl), borrowed from Arabic أَصْل (ʔaṣl).

def: s.m. pl. (of سلف salaf), Those who have gone before; ancestors.
adj. compar. & superl. (of صالح ṣālěh), Better; best; most correct; most advisable.
adv. Ever, at any time, at all; by any means; in the least; absolutely; entirely, wholly, altogether
subs: (with neg.), adv. By no means, absolutely never.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اصلاً (aslan), from Arabic أَصْلًا (ʔaṣlan).

def: s.m. (f. in Sanskrit), The ninth Nakshatra or lunar mansion — five stars near the southern claw of Cancer.
s.m. Embracing, embrace, intwining; intimate connection.
s.m. (f. in Sanskrit), The ninth Nakshatra or lunar mansion — five stars near the southern claw of Cancer.
s.f. Original land, exclusive of subsequent additions, as from alluvial deposits
subs: s.f. Original value or price; real value
s.m. The original or chief village, of which other villages are the offshoots, and that on which the revenue was originally assessed.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَصْلِی (aslī), borrowed from Arabic أَصْلِيّ (ʔaṣliyy), from أَصْل (ʔaṣl) + ـِيّ (-iyy).

def: s.m. Arsenal, magazine, armoury ( = silāḥ-ḵẖāna).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَسْلِحَه (asliha), from Arabic أَسْلِحَة (ʔasliḥa), plural of سِلَاح (silāḥ, “weapon”).

def: s.f. Originality, essentiality, reality, genuineness, pureness, nobleness.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَصْلِیَّت (asliyyat), from Arabic أَصْلِيَّة (ʔaṣliyya), equivalent to اَصْل (asl) + ـیَت (-iyat).

def: s.m. Verse, stanza; couplet, distich, poetry; hymn of praise; panegyric
subs: s.f. A present from the female relatives of the bride to the bridegroom for reciting some verses called ćhan (syn. g̠azal-paṛhwāʼī).

def: adv. = اصلا aṣlaṉ, q.v. adv. Ever, at any time, at all; by any means; in the least; absolutely; entirely, wholly, altogether
subs: (with neg.), adv. By no means, absolutely never.

def: = آسمان āsmān, q.v. s.m. Sky, firmament; heaven, the celestial orb; the vault or canopy of heaven
subs: Lit. 'To spit at the sky'; to act improperly or foolishly; to kick against the pricks
v.t. To exalt to the skies; to speak highly of, to praise extravagantly; to flatter, wheedle, coax
To exalt oneself to the skies, to be very conceited, vain or proud; to affect greatness, give oneself airs

āsmān jhāṅknā, v.n. Lit. 'To turn the eye to the sky' (as a game cock, in the pride of his strength is supposed to do); to feel the pride of strength; to wax strong and bold; to be in good form (for fighting, &c.)
v.n. Lit. 'the sky to fall upon'; to be overtaken by misfortune, to be overwhelmed with trouble
adj. Of exalted dignity, very exalted, of great eminence or dignity
āsmān-se ṭakkar khānā, To reach to the sky; to vie with the sky (in height); to be very high; to exceed in height or appearance
To drop from the sky, fall from the clouds; to be obtained unexpectedly; to be gained without labour or exertion; to be undervalued; to be regarded as worthless or contemptible
adj. Vast as the firmament, grand as the firmament
s.m. Anything very lofty or high or tall (as a pole, man, &c.); a hukka, the stem of which reaches from the lower to the upper floor of a house
Lit. 'To break the stars of the sky'; to be very skilful or expert in a business or trade
(or -gīrī), s.f. Lit. 'Catching or intercepting the sky'; projecting roof; canopy, awning; arbour, bower
Lit. 'To pierce the sky'; to be very skilful or expert at a business or trade ( = āsmān-ke tāre toṛnā).

def: s.m. pl. (of ism), Names
subs: asmā-i-ʻuz̤mā, The ninety-nine names or epithets of God.

def: adj. Heavenly, celestial; from heaven, divine; sky-coloured, azure, cerulean; unexpected, sudden
subs: (-par), āsmānī g̠aẓab ṭūṭnā (-par), To be overtaken by a calamity from heaven, or by divine vengeance
To make (a person) intoxicated (especially with bhang)
s.m. An arrow shot towards the sky, an arrow shot at random; an arrow shot by an unknown hand; a profitless venture; an useless act

s.f. Lit. 'Heavenly decree'; unforeseen disasters, failure of crops, &c. (arising from drought or excessive rain); a phrase formerly used in deeds and leases as a provision against loss or injury arising from calamitous seasons, or unjust exactions of the Government, which, if affecting the zamīndārs, the ryots were compelled to make good.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آسمانی (āsmānī). Equivalent to آسمان (āsmān, “sky, celestial sphere”) + ـی (-ī).

def: s.m. pl. (of سمر samar), Discourses by night; tales related in the night for amusement.
s.m. Forgetting, forgetfulness, oblivion.
s.f. Strangury, stone or gravel (the disease).
s.f. Want of memory, forgetfulness.
adv. In the mean time, meanwhile, in the interim or interval; upon this.
wety: Arabic أَثْنَاء (ʔaṯnāʔ).

def: s.f. (m. ?) pl. (of سند sanad), Grants, deeds, charters, law papers in general, documents, vouchers, warrants, authorities
s.f. pl. (of صنف ṣinf), Sorts, kinds, varieties.
s.m. Bathing, ablution, purification; (also written asnān)
subs: s.m. Bathing and religious meditation, daily worship (of a Hindū).

def: s.m. Bathing.
wety: From Sanskrit स्नान (snāna).

def: s.m. Want of affection, unkindness; — adj. Wanting in affection, unkind, harsh.
s.f. Want of beauty or elegance, ugliness; — adj. Shapeless, ill-made, ugly, inelegant.
s.f. = اسوبها asobhā, q.v. s.f. Want of beauty or elegance, ugliness; — adj. Shapeless, ill-made, ugly, inelegant.

adj. Impossible to be effected by reflection or study, not contrivable; inconceivable, unimaginable; thoughtless, inconsiderate, inattentive, negligent, regardless, careless, heedless, indifferent; imprudent; — s.m. Absence of care, tranquillity, content.

s.m. Absence of care, &c. = اسوچ asoć, q.v. adj. s.m. Ease, tranquillity, comfort; cheerfulness ( = asok); — the tree Uvaria longifolia (syn. deva-dāru).
adj. Undisturbed, unmolested, quiet, tranquil, calm, at ease, comfortable.
s.m. The sixth solar month ( = āsin and kuwār).
s.m. Freedom from care or sorrow; ease, &c. ( = asog, q.v.); — the tree Jonesia asoca; adj. At ease, unmolested, tranquil, cheerful.
s.m. & adj. = اسوك asok and asog, q.v. The tree Jonesia aśoka
subs: (aśoka+ashṭamī), s.m. The eighth day in the first half of the month Ćait (April-May), when a festival in honour of Viśnu is observed, part of the ceremonial of which consists in drinking water with the buds of Asoka in it.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit अशोक (aśoka).

def: = اسوگی asogī, q.v. adj. Undisturbed, unmolested, quiet, tranquil, calm, at ease, comfortable.
adv. This year ( = is-sāl, q.v.s.v. is).

ety: Pehl. aśp, aśb; Zend aśpa; S. अश्व
def: s.m. Horse; the knight (at chess)
ex: وَزِیرِ زَماں نے لِیا ایک اَسْپ کِہ ہَے رَشْکِ گُلْگُون بادِ بَہار نَظَر پوسْت سے اُس کے آتا ہَے خُوں
vazīr-i zamā̃ ne liyā ek asp ki hai raśk-i gulgūn bād-i bahār nazar post se us ke ātā hai xū̃
The vizier of the time had gotten a horse, the sight of which rivals the rose-colored bād-i bahār (a speedy Mughal chariot). Blood comes out of its hide.
سَجے ہُوئے ہَیں پیادَہ و اَسْپ و فِیل تَمام۔ بِچھی ہُوئی ہَے بِساط جَہاں کَہاں گَیا مَیں۔
saje hūe ha͠i pyāda o asp o fīl tamām. bichī hūī hai bisāt jahā̃ kahā̃ gayā ma͠i.
All the pawn and knight and bishop (or alfil) are set / Where the chess cloth is spread / Where did I go?
wety: PIE word *h₁éḱwos Borrowed from Classical Persian اسپ (asp) from Middle Persian 𐭮𐭥𐭮𐭩𐭠 (sʿsyʾ /⁠asp⁠/) from Old Persian 𐎠𐎿𐎱 (a-s-p /⁠aspa⁠/, “horse”). Cognate with Baluchi اسپ (asp), Pashto اس (as), Kalasha hãš, and Shina اںشپو (ä̃špo).

def: adj. Of or belonging to a horse.
s.m. Place, situation; footing; dignity, rank.
s.m. Lit. 'Horse's ear'; seed of the fleawort or plaintain, Plantago ispaghul. (The leaf of the plant is, in shape, somewhat like a horse's ear — whence the name).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اسپغول (ispaġōl).

def: s.f. 'A little horse'; bridge of a musical instrument.
s.m. = اسپرش asparś, q.v.
s.m. The state of a Hindū after bathing, previous to worship or eating, during which it is unlawful for him to touch any one. (See next.)
part. adj. Not to be touched; literally or ceremonially impure.
adj. = اسپرشي asparśī, q.v.

ety: S. स्पर्शिन्, the अ a being redundant
def: adj. Tangible, palpable.
adj. & s.m. Intangible, impalpable; — anything intangible.
s.m. Vicinity, neighbourhood, proximity; neighbours; companions; — adv. & postpos. Around, about, on every side; in the vicinity (of -ke); near, hard by (syn. ird-gird)
subs: adj. (f. -ī), Adjacent, adjoining, neighbouring; living or being near.
wety: Reduplication of پاس (pās, “near”).

def: adj. Indistinct; unintelligible.
part. adj. = asparśanīya, q.v. part. adj. Not to be touched; literally or ceremonially impure.
s.m. Time, a space or period of time, an age; — afternoon; — the afternoon prayer (between 4.30 and sunset = aṣr-kī namāz).
s.m. Corner; hair of the head; blood; tear.

s.m. Abode, asylum, place of refuge, shelter, retreat, hiding-place; means of defence, protection or security, defence, safeguard, safety, security, surety; support, stay; protector, patron; hope, expectation, trust, reliance, confidence, dependence, assurance; help, means of subsistence
subs: (-kā), āsrā johnā (-kā), āsrā ḍhūṅḍhnā -(-kā), To look for help or support (from), seek succour (from), expect the support (of); to look (for), watch (for), be in expectation (of)
(-kā), To lose (one's) hope, to be disappointed in (one's) expectations; to despair
(-ko), To give hope, to raise expectations; to console; to give assurance, inspire confidence, to reassure; to encourage
(-kā), To rest, rely, depend or lean (on), to be dependent (on); to rest or feel assured; to count (on), trust (in), confide (in); to hope, expect
(-kā, or meṅ), To place trust (in); to seek the support (of); to seek the shelter (of), take refuge (in)
(-kā), To be the support, or stay, or prop, or refuge (of).
wety: From Sanskrit आश्रय (āśraya).

def: s.m. Plea, excuse; deceit, fraud, cheating, circumvention; — abode, retreat, &c. = آسرا āsrā, q.v.
s.m. A tear ( = asru, q.v.) s.m. A tear.
wety: From Sanskrit अश्रु (áśru), from Proto-Indo-European *dáḱru-. Cognate with Ancient Greek δάκρυ (dákru), Lithuanian ašara.

def: s.f. Want of confidence in, distrust, disbelief; dislike, aversion, disgust, loathing, contempt.
s.f. = اسردها asraddhā, q.v. s.f. Want of confidence in, distrust, disbelief; dislike, aversion, disgust, loathing, contempt.
part. adj. Not to be believed, incredible.

adj. Hopeful, trusting, confident; — s.m. Refugee, &c. = āsrit, q.v. part. adj. Resorting (to); dependent (on); — s.m. One who has recourse to or relies on another; refugee; protegée; dependant, follower, subject, servant, retainer; hanger on, parasite, sycophant; — a Brahman who assists in the performance of marriage ceremonies.

s.m. = آسريت āsrait, q.v. adj. Hopeful, trusting, confident; — s.m. Refugee, &c. = āsrit, q.v. part. adj. Resorting (to); dependent (on); — s.m. One who has recourse to or relies on another; refugee; protegée; dependant, follower, subject, servant, retainer; hanger on, parasite, sycophant; — a Brahman who assists in the performance of marriage ceremonies.

s.m. Abode, residence; the abode of a hermit, or of a religious student; hermitage; college, school; — a class or religious order (of which the Hindūs reckon four, referable to the different periods of a Brāhman's life, viz. 1˚ The Brahmāćārī, who devotes his life to religious exercises, aūsterity, and celibacy; 2˚ The Gr̤ihī or Gr̤ihastha, who lives in the world and rears a family; 3˚ The Vānaprastha, who retires from the world with his family and passes his life in devotion in the forest; 4˚ The Bhikshu or Sannyasī, who subsists on alms).

s.m. = آسرم āsram, q.v. s.m. Abode, residence; the abode of a hermit, or of a religious student; hermitage; college, school; — a class or religious order (of which the Hindūs reckon four, referable to the different periods of a Brāhman's life, viz. 1˚ The Brahmāćārī, who devotes his life to religious exercises, aūsterity, and celibacy; 2˚ The Gr̤ihī or Gr̤ihastha, who lives in the world and rears a family; 3˚ The Vānaprastha, who retires from the world with his family and passes his life in devotion in the forest; 4˚ The Bhikshu or Sannyasī, who subsists on alms).
adj. Belonging to one of the four orders mentioned above.
adj. = آسرمي āsramī, q.v. adj. Belonging to one of the four orders mentioned above.
s.m. pl. (of سر sirr), Secrets; confidential concerns.

adj. & s.m. Unarmed, defenceless; — an unarmed person.

def: s.f. Praise, eulogy, commendation, celebration, glorification, adoration, worship, invocation, prayer; hymn, anthem
subs: (-kī), To praise, bless, glorify, invoke, hymn the praise (of).

def: s.f. Absence of applause; dispraise.
s.m. Breath, life; the five vital breaths or airs of the body; reflection, thought, or the heart as the seat of it; affection; grief.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀏𑀲𑁄 (eso), from Sanskrit एषः (eṣaḥ).

def: adv. Quickly, speedily, swiftly, directly, forthwith
subs: adj. Easily pleased or satisfied, soon appeased.

def: adj. Unfavourable, inauspicious, unlucky, bad.
adj. Unlucky, inauspicious, unpropitious, unfavourable; unfortunate; evil, bad; unbecoming, inelegant; — s.m. Inauspiciousness; unhappiness; unfavourableness; misfortune, calamity, evil; distress.
s.f. Impure, unclean, foul; — impurity; pollution.

= اشوچ aśoć, q.v. adj. Impossible to be effected by reflection or study, not contrivable; inconceivable, unimaginable; thoughtless, inconsiderate, inattentive, negligent, regardless, careless, heedless, indifferent; imprudent; — s.m. Absence of care, tranquillity, content.
( = S. अशुचि, rt. शुच्) = اسچ asuć, q.v.
s.f. (see asuć), Impurity, foulness, pollution.

ety: perf. part. of āsūdan; (ā+rt. Zend. śā; S. शा, शो
def: part. adj. Satisfied, contented; satiated, full, glutted; easy, at ease, tranquil, peaceful, comfortable, possessing a competency; affluent; — adv. In easy circumstances (in respect of, -se), well-off, well-to-do
subs: āsūda-dil, adj. Of calm or composed mind, composed in mind, contented, calm, tranquil, serene.

def: s.m.f. pl. (of āsūda), Those at rest or peace, the dead.
s.f. Contentment, ease, quiet, tranquillity, calm, peace; comfort, well-being, easy circumstances.
adj. = اشدهہ aśuddh, q.v.
adj. Impure; corrupt (as language or style); erroneous, inaccurate, incorrect, wrong.
s.f. Uncleanness, impurity; inaccuracy, incorrectness, error.
s.m. One who speaks, reads, or writes inaccurately.
